expectations.text: Mentions dhat crate or counting allocator for measurement
expectations.evidence: Output uses dhat exclusively. Sets up #[global_allocator] static ALLOC: dhat::Alloc = dhat::Alloc; and dhat::Profiler::builder().testing().build(). Also adds dhat = '0.3' to Cargo.toml dev-dependencies.

expectations.text: Shows how to set up a memory test or profiling
expectations.evidence: Provides complete integration test file (pdf_memory_budget.rs) with realistic data generation, HeapStats assertions, and exact run command (cargo test --release --test pdf_memory_budget -- --nocapture).

expectations.text: Considers concurrency multiplier (concurrent requests × per-call memory)
expectations.evidence: Includes a dedicated 'pdf_export_concurrent_simulation' test that runs 5 sequential calls and states: 'In production each request would hold its own buffer simultaneously. This test shows per-call allocation; multiply by expected concurrency.' Decision table shows per-call × 10 concurrent.

expectations.text: References the pod memory limit in the decision
expectations.evidence: Decision framework table explicitly compares against '384Mi budget'. Also mentions 'Baseline RSS of your backend process. Subtract this from 384Mi to get your actual headroom.'

expectations.text: Does NOT guess the memory usage without measuring
expectations.evidence: Final section states: 'I am not going to predict what the numbers will be. The skill's entire purpose is to replace guessing with measurement.' No memory estimates appear anywhere.
